description: A South Yarra courtyard converted from paving and lawn to a layered native garden. Lomandra, Dianella and Poa groundcover established across a compact 45m2 site. Ecological baseline now documented and the garden is building toward its first fauna sighting.

activity_log.title: Baseline assessment -- urban ecological retrofit
activity_log.notes: Baseline condition recorded. Initial score set to establish trajectory tracking. Priority signals: structure, refuge, soil readiness, and water-wise function.

activity_log.title: Rear Garden -- Calm Immersion
activity_log.notes: Beth's garden has been designed as a place of calm immersion -- a space where cottage softness meets Japanese-inspired restraint. The canopy of Acer palmatum and dwarf lemon-scented gum creates seasonal presence and dappled light, while sculptural forms like Doryanthes, Hakea and soft drifts of Banksia and Correa anchor the space with structure and habitat value. Beneath this framework, a tapestry of everlastings, billy buttons, flannel flowers and mauve daisies bring pink and purple tones through the seasons, many chosen for their ability to dry beautifully for preserving. The result is a resilient, low-maintenance garden that feels expressive yet grounded -- a place to read, illustrate, forage, hang laundry in the breeze, and quietly lose yourself in beauty year-round.

activity_log.title: Front Garden -- Soft Meadow Arrival
activity_log.notes: The front garden has been conceived as a soft meadow arrival -- a gentle, textural landscape with movement and seasonal rhythm. Repurposed bricks are reset into a small meandering path, creating a sense of wander rather than directness, while improving permeability and grounding the space in material honesty. Grasses such as Poa cita provide structure and sway, forming a luminous backdrop for wind flowers, globe thistles and Jerusalem sage. Lavender, catmint and society garlic weave through in mauve and soft purple tones, echoing Beth's love of colour while maintaining resilience. The result is a meadow that feels expressive and romantic but controlled -- a welcoming threshold that shifts beautifully through the seasons.
